places.name: The Echo Park Time Travel Mart
places.description: Non-profit time travel mart sells goods you would need for a trip through the fourth dimension.

places.name: The Last Bookstore
places.description: This iconic L.A. bookshop is housed in an abandoned bank—both symbolic and chic.

places.name: The Museum of Death
places.description: World's largest collection of serial killer artwork and other macabre exhibits.

places.name: Sunken City
places.description: Remants of foundations, streets, and streetcar tracks inhabit the cliffs where a landslide occurred in 1929.

places.name: Bradbury Building
places.description: The legendary building that needed ghostly approval before being built.

places.name: Griffith Observatory's Tesla Coil
places.description: High on a hill overlooking Los Angeles lives a world famous, high power conical Tesla Coil.

places.name: Old Zoo Picnic Area
places.description: The abandoned zoo enclosures offer a perfect place to experience the other side of the bars.

places.name: Galco's Soda Pop Stop
places.description: Shop selling 500 flavors of soda has something for everyone.

places.name: Tiki-Ti
places.description: Established in the '60s, a family-owned tropical drink bar founded by Ray Buhen, bartender to the stars.

places.name: Pink's Hot Dogs
places.description: World-famous, Pink's still manages to keep its prized cult fetish status.
